fields.name: videoId
fields.label: YouTube video ID
fields.description: The 11-character ID from a YouTube URL (e.g. dQw4w9WgXcQ)

fields.name: seoTitle
fields.label: Meta Title (SEO)
fields.description: Shown in the browser tab and search results — not on the page itself. To change the heading visitors see at the top of the page, edit the Headline of the page's Hero block (if it has one) in Page Sections below.

fields.name: blocks
fields.label: Page Sections
fields.description: The visible content of the page. When the page starts with a Hero block, its Headline is the main on-page heading — edit that to change what visitors see at the top.

defaultItem.tagline: Here's some text above the other text
defaultItem.headline: Astro + TinaCMS, ready to ship

defaultItem.text: Support for live editing and editorial workflow

defaultItem.title: Built to cover your needs
defaultItem.description: Everything you need to build content-driven sites.

defaultItem.title: Start Building
defaultItem.description: Get started with TinaCMS today and take your content management to the next level.

defaultItem.body: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetuer adipiscing elit. Donec odio.

defaultItem.quote: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things.
defaultItem.author: Phil Karlton

testimonials.quote: There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things.
testimonials.author: Phil Karlton

defaultItem.title: A headline that sits beside your image
defaultItem.body: Describe the feature or story here, with a supporting image right alongside it.

fields.name: seo
fields.label: Site Identity & SEO
fields.description: Site-wide identity. These values appear on every page — the Site Name is shown in the header navigation and used as the default browser title; the Description is the default for search results and social shares.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Site Name
fields.description: Shown in the header navigation on every page. Lives in Global Config because it's the same site-wide — each page sets its own browser title via the Meta Title field on the page, and this Site Name is used as the fallback if a page is ever missing one.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Default Meta Description (SEO)
fields.description: Default description shown in search results and social-sharing previews when a page does not provide its own.

fields.name: siteOwner
fields.label: Site Owner (shown in footer)
fields.description: Your name or company name. Displayed in the site footer.

fields.name: logo
fields.label: Logo
fields.description: Shown next to the Site Name in the header navigation.

fields.name: nav
fields.label: Navigation Menu
fields.description: Links shown in the header navigation. Reorder, add, or remove items below. The Site Name shown to the left of these links is set in Site Identity & SEO above.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Link Label
fields.description: The text shown in the nav for this link.

fields.name: link
fields.label: Link URL
fields.description: Where this nav item points (e.g. /about or https://example.com).

fields.name: icon
fields.label: Icon
fields.description: Any Tabler icon name, e.g. tabler:brand-x, tabler:book-2, tabler:brand-github. Browse at https://icones.js.org/collection/tabler

description.value: References another document, used as a foreign key
